whatItDoes: Converts any text into the NATO phonetic alphabet, where each letter is replaced by its standard NATO word (A→Alpha, B→Bravo, etc.) and digits use standard pronunciation (0→Zero, 9→Niner). Output is displayed one word per line.
whyItExists: Spelling out codes, passwords, or identifiers over the phone or radio is error-prone. The NATO phonetic alphabet eliminates ambiguity by assigning a unique word to each letter.
whoShouldUse: Customer support agents reading verification codes, military and aviation personnel, developers sharing API keys or tokens verbally, and anyone who needs to spell out strings clearly.
useCases: Spell out a customer's verification code over the phone without confusion
Convert a password or API key to phonetic words for verbal sharing
Teach or learn the NATO phonetic alphabet interactively
Prepare phonetic spellings for aviation or military communications
instructions: Type or paste any text into the input field
Each letter is instantly converted to its NATO phonetic word
Spaces between letters are shown as dashes for clarity
Click Copy to copy the phonetic output to your clipboard
examples: HELLO → Hotel, Echo, Lima, Lima, Oscar
ABC123 → Alpha, Bravo, Charlie, One, Two, Three
bestPractices: Use one word per line for easy reading aloud
Digits are converted using NATO standard pronunciation (Niner for 9)
Non-alphanumeric characters are passed through unchanged
commonMistakes: Confusing NATO phonetic words with other phonetic alphabets like the old US military one
Expecting lowercase output — NATO phonetic words are always capitalized
Forgetting that spaces become dashes in the output
faq: Does this use the current NATO standard alphabet? — Yes, it uses the ICAO/NATO standard phonetic alphabet.
What happens to numbers? — They use standard pronunciation: Zero, One, Two, ..., Niner.
Can I use this for aviation or military comms? — Yes, the words match the official standard.
